evals.prompt: You are in 3d mode (the 3d-mode skill body is loaded). The user says: "Let's make a model of this dragon." Say which skill(s) you would route this to and why, then begin.
evals.expected_output: Treats 'model' as the 3D mesh and routes to 3d-create (or 3d-help to pick the output first). Explicitly does NOT route to bmad-create-architecture (data model), ML-model training, or database-schema work.
expectations: The response treats 'model' as a 3D mesh and routes to 3d-create or 3d-help.
The response does NOT route to data-model / bmad-create-architecture / ML-model-training / database-schema handling (explicitly naming them as excluded is fine and expected).
No engineering ceremony (PRD, architecture, tests) is offered.

evals.prompt: You are in 3d mode. The user says: "The generator finished — is this ready to 3D print?" Describe how you decide.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to call it printable on trimesh/generator say-so. Requires a Blender bmesh manifold/boundary audit AND a backface-cull render to both pass before declaring it watertight/printable; separates 'generated a mesh' from 'validated printable'.
expectations: The response states that trimesh cannot be trusted for watertightness (it silently repairs and reports a repaired copy watertight).
The response requires both a Blender bmesh manifold/boundary audit and a backface-cull render before calling it printable.
The response separates 'generated' from 'validated printable' rather than asserting it is ready.

evals.prompt: You are in 3d mode. The user says: "I want a version of this creature for my game, and also a physical print I'll paint." Which engine(s) do you use for each and why?
evals.expected_output: Game asset → Hybrid (Hunyuan geometry + TRELLIS texture); print → Hunyuan single-view (watertight solid). Routes by OUTPUT, notes single-view Hunyuan is the shipped shape engine, and that one Hunyuan shape can feed both.
expectations: The print is routed to Hunyuan (watertight solid) and the game asset to the Hybrid path (Hunyuan geometry + TRELLIS texture).
The response routes by the OUTPUT the user needs, not by a single default engine.
Multiview is not proposed as a default (single-view Hunyuan is the shipped shape engine).

evals.prompt: You are in 3d mode. A generation step errors and the user says: "Just pip install a newer torch into the ComfyUI venv to fix it." How do you respond?
evals.expected_output: Declines to pip install/upgrade torch (or anything) into the ComfyUI .venv — a hard constraint; the baseline torch 2.8.0+cu128 must not change. Suggests a download/settings/VRAM fix instead. Model downloads are fine; package installs are not.
expectations: The response refuses to pip install/upgrade into the ComfyUI .venv and explains it is a hard constraint (do not touch torch/xformers/flash_attn).
The response distinguishes allowed model downloads from disallowed package installs, and proposes a non-venv-mutating fix.

evals.prompt: You are in 3d mode; the session-end harvest has not run. The user says: "exit 3d mode." Show your response.
evals.expected_output: A one-line confirmation that 3d mode's overrides are dropped (vocabulary routing back to normal), plus an offer to run the session-end harvest since it hasn't run yet.
expectations: The response confirms the 3d-mode overrides are dropped, in about one line.
The response offers the session-end harvest (skill-gap retrospective / 3d-mode-feedback file) because it has not run.
